name: contains_out_of_upper_bound
code:
proof fn phi_1_contains_out_of_upper_bound(mask: CommitMask)
    requires
        mask@ == Set::new(|i: int| 0 <= i < COMMIT_MASK_BITS),
    ensures
        mask@.contains(512int),
{
}
reason: After create_full the set should be {0..511}; containing 512 would mean the upper bound is wrong

name: contains_negative_index
code:
proof fn phi_2_contains_negative_index(mask: CommitMask)
    requires
        mask@ == Set::new(|i: int| 0 <= i < COMMIT_MASK_BITS),
    ensures
        mask@.contains(-1int),
{
}
reason: After create_full the set should have no negative elements; containing -1 would indicate a lower-bound error

name: result_set_is_empty
code:
proof fn phi_3_result_set_is_empty(mask: CommitMask)
    requires
        mask@ == Set::new(|i: int| 0 <= i < COMMIT_MASK_BITS),
    ensures
        mask@ =~= Set::empty(),
{
}
reason: create_full should produce a full mask; being empty would mean the spec is trivially unsound or vacuous

name: result_equals_half_range
code:
proof fn phi_4_result_equals_half_range(mask: CommitMask)
    requires
        mask@ == Set::new(|i: int| 0 <= i < COMMIT_MASK_BITS),
    ensures
        mask@ =~= Set::new(|i: int| 0 <= i < 256),
{
}
reason: The full mask covers 512 bits; equalling only the first 256 would indicate COMMIT_MASK_BITS is misused or halved

name: missing_last_element
code:
proof fn phi_5_missing_last_element(mask: CommitMask)
    requires
        mask@ == Set::new(|i: int| 0 <= i < COMMIT_MASK_BITS),
    ensures
        !mask@.contains(511int),
{
}
reason: 511 is the last valid index in a 512-bit mask; its absence would indicate an off-by-one in the spec
